instruction:
There is a popular story (among data miners) that there is a correlation between men buying diapers and buying beer while shopping. A student tests this theory by surveying 132 male shoppers as they left a grocery store. The results are summarized in the contingency table. Test for a dependent relationship between buying beer and buying diapers. Conduct this test at the 0.01 significance level.
$\begin{array}{cccc}\hline & Bought Diapers & Did Not Buy Diapers & Totals \\ \hline Beer & 7 & 49 & 56 \\ \hline No Beer & 10 & 66 & 76 \\ \hline Totals & 17 & 115 & 132 \\ \hline \end{array}$ 132
(a) Find the expected frequencies.
$\begin{array}{ccc}\hline & Bought Diapers & Did Not Buy Diapers \\ \hline Beer & [ANS] & [ANS] \\ \hline No Beer & [ANS] & [ANS] \\ \hline \end{array}$ (b) Find the test statistic. [ANS]
(c) Find the critical value. [ANS]
(d) Is there sufficient data to support the claim? [ANS] [ANS]
